q: The ___ effect is the change in frequency of a wave related to an observer.
Doppler effect is due to the ___ motion between the source and observer.
The Doppler effect is named after the Austrian physicist ___ Doppler.
Christian Doppler described the Doppler phenomenon in ___.
Usually, only the motion of the source and observer directly towards or ___ from each other is considered.
A common example of Doppler shift is the change of frequency (___) heard from a vehicle horn.
In the Doppler equation, $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$, $f_o$ is frequency heard by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $f_s$ is frequency produced by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v$ is the speed of sound in the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v_o$ is speed of the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v_s$ is speed of the ___.
When source and observer are approaching, $f_o ___ f_s$.
When source and observer are receding, $f_o ___ f_s$.
$+ v_o$ is used when the observer moves ___ the source.
$- v_o$ is used when the observer moves ___ from the source.
$- v_s$ is used when the source moves ___ the observer.
$+ v_s$ is used when the source moves ___ from the observer.
Doppler effect is observed not only in sound but also in ___ radiations.
The wavelength of light from a star observed from earth shifts due to Doppler effect towards the ___ end of the spectrum.
The phenomenon where light shifts toward lower frequency and longer wavelength is called ___ shift.
If a star were approaching the earth, spectral lines would shift towards ___ end.
___ (radio detection and ranging) is a form of echolocation that uses electromagnetic waves.
Radar stands for ___ detection and ranging.
Radar uses ___ waves instead of sound.
Doppler ultrasound is a technique used to examine ___ flow.
The technique of measuring shift in ultrasound reflected by red blood cells is known as ___.
Doppler ultrasound can reveal blockage to blood flow or formation of ___ in arteries.
Doppler ultrasound can provide detailed information on the heartbeat of the ___ during labor.
___ can be used to examine an organ such as the heart.
